DISCOVERY 525, PLAINTIFF ROMELL JORDAN AN INDIVIDUAL NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET TWO IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. WITHIN TEN (10) CALENDAR DAYS, LEAD COUNSEL FOR THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON, OR VIA ZOOM OR SIMILAR PLATFORM (WITH CAMERA ON), REGARDING THE REQUESTS AND RESPONSES/OBJECTIONS AT ISSUE. PLAINTIFF WILL HAVE THIRTY (30) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THE MEET-AND-CONFER MEETING TO AMEND/REFILE THE MOTION WITH A DETAILED DECLARATION OF WHAT AGREEMENTS, IF ANY, WERE ARRIVED AT DURING THE MEETING. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURTS RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO JUDGE PRO TEM JOHN-PAUL DEOL AT JPDEOL@DHILLONLAW.COM. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two
Off Calendar Oct-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Danja Stocca, Esq./ Kiesha Payne. Esq. QUILL & ARROW, LLP (310) 933-4271 dstocca@quillarrowlaw.com Attorney for Plaintiff, ROMELL JORDAN
Paige S. Silva, Esq., EVANS FEARS SCHUTTERT MCNULTY MICKUS (949) 301-9464 silva@efsmmlaw.com
/ efsmmhondaconsumer@efsmmlaw.com Attorneys for Defendant AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC.

Matter is not contested. All parties submit to the Court's tentative ruling and are deemd to have stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem.

No hearing/argumment is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ROMELL JORDAN AN INDIVIDUAL Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two

The motion is denied without prejudice. Within ten (10) calendar days, lead counsel for the parties are ordered to meet and confer in person, or via Zoom or similar platform (with camera on), regarding the requests and responses/objections at issue. Plaintiff will have thirty (30) calendar days from the date of the meet-and-confer meeting to amend/refile the motion with a detailed declaration of what agreements, if any, were arrived at during the meeting.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Courts ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem John-Paul Deol at JPDeol@dhillonlaw.com

Judge Pro Tem: John-Paul Deol; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (525/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance And Production Of Documents By Defendant'S Person(S) Most Qualified On Matters Not Provided, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-16-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF ROMELL JORDAN'S, AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance And Production Of Documents By Defendant'S Person(S) Most Qualified On Matters Not Provided, And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Larry Castruita, Esq., of Castruita Law Firm, PC, (949) 535-1800, for Plaintiff.
Paige Silva, Esq., of Evans Fears Schuttert McNulty Mickus, (702) 901-7050, for Defendant.

Counsels stipulate to the subject matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem and authorize the clerk to execute the formal stipulation on their behalf.

The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court grants Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance And Production Of Documents By Defendant's Person(s) Most Qualified On Matters Not Provided. Plaintiff's Request For Monetary Sanctions is denied without prejudice.

Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order in accordance with the discussion at today's hearing, approved as to form, and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem to schwartz@cmslaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Andy Schwartz. Clerk: R. Woods. Court Reporter: Jennifer Roux, CSR: 11033, T: (707) 477-9599, E: jenrouxcsr@gmail.com. (525/JPT)
